rulings.Case Title: YOSHABEL CLEMENTS VS. SOJOURN PROPERTIES INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, December 30, 2013, line 10. DEFENDANT SOJOURN PROPERTIES INC., STEVEN KING MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Prosecution is GRANTED. Defendant shifted its burden to show that Plaintiff's complaint arises out of protected activity, i.e. filing and maintaining unlawful detainer action CUD-11-639673. Plaintiff failed to file timely opposition on November 14, 2013. Instead, Plaintiff filed and served by mail First Amended Complaint on November 15, 2013. Plaintiff's piecemeal opposition filed on December 16, 19, 23, and 27 2013 is stricken as untimely. Even if opposition was considered, Plaintiff fails to sustain its burden under the second prong of CCP 425.16 and show a probability of prevailing on the merits on the Complaint. Plaintiff may not amend the complaint before the hearing on anti-SLAPP motion. Salma v. Capon (2008) 161 Cal.App.4th 1275.Plaitff' reliance on Lam v. Ngo (2001) 91 Cal.App.4th 832 for the proposition that First Amended Complaint is the operative pleading under the circumstances is misplaced. In Lam, First Amended Complaint was filed before anti-SLAPP and the issue was timeliness of the anti-SLAPP motion. Attorneys' fees and costs granted in the amount of $3,400. = (501/HMD)

rulings.Case Title: DON LEVISON VS. KEVIN BARNARD et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, December 30, 2013, line 7. DEFENDANT TARYN BARNARD DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. OVERRULED, 5 days to answer.
